commit_scope: Closes the linux-as-recipes signals + interrupts rows with ONE engine: afferent-offer.fk carries the unsolicited afferent offer (AO-OFFER line/at/payload), a handler table and mask that are cells (AO-ENTRY/AO-TABLE/AO-MASK), and ao-wait, a wait-with-deadline acknowledging with the {nothing, node} arms of axiom-5 — no event in the window IS nothing (axiom-1, timeout==nothing). Signals and interrupts are two thin data readings of the same engine: ao-alarm (a timer armed for tick T is a standing offer on the ALRM line — SIGALRM and timeout are one structure) and ao-irq (a device event latches a payload on a line; masked line = offer not acknowledged). Content-addressing gives POSIX standard-signal coalescing for free; ao-consume completes delivery.

local_validation.summary: Band verdict 511 (9 claims), 1 ok, 0 divergent — Go = Rust = TypeScript. Both readings through the one ao-wait engine: a timer armed AT the deadline delivers on-alarm while one tick past acknowledges NOTHING (the window edge pinned in one tick — timeout==nothing IS the SIGALRM shape, checked); a masked SIGINT stays pending and the SAME pending delivers once the mask lifts (mask as data, not control flow); a keyboard IRQ dispatches with its latched scancode and node_eq correlation to the originating offer; a silent line and an unoffered line both acknowledge nothing; the signal-timeout nothing and the irq-silence nothing are the SAME NodeID; consuming the delivered alarm makes the next wait nothing (one-shot, coalesced).

observed_delta.blueprints_registered: AO-OFFER/AO-ENTRY/AO-TABLE/AO-MASK/AO-DISPATCH/AO-NOTHING at 1.2.99.1940-1945; bp tables regenerated for all three kernels
observed_delta.ledger_rows_now_runnable: linux-as-recipes (signals) and (interrupts) — coordinator flips NAMED to RUNS after sibling merges

known_limitations.limitation: Ticks are data (a logical clock), not yet fed by op 15 TIME; the band is pure and deterministic by design.
known_limitations.follow_up: A host carrier where op 15 TIME feeds real ticks and a fourth-kernel select/poll witness shows a live timeout yielding nothing — named in afferent-offer.form, built when an ask needs it.

known_limitations.limitation: Delivery order is pending order; POSIX number-order and IRQ priority are policy cells over the same engine, not yet authored.
known_limitations.follow_up: Priority-as-data when a consumer needs it.

ci_validation.summary: PR gates run the three-way bands; sibling kernels keep the verdict honest.

deploy_validation.summary: form-stdlib rides the api rebuild; the lattice auto-ingests afferent-offer.form on merge.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: No route changes. The stdlib gains the unsolicited-afferent-offer engine; signals and interrupts move from NAMED to runnable recipe + band.
test_flows: afferent-offer-band three-way: SIGALRM edge, timeout==nothing, sigprocmask pending, unmask delivery, IRQ scancode dispatch, silent line, unoffered door, shared nothing-cell, consume.

phase_gate.gate: both ledger rows carried by one engine with a three-way band; ledger flip stays with the coordinator
phase_gate.next: coordinator flips linux-as-recipes rows after all five sibling PRs merge; live-tick carrier when asked
